chapter_title.ar: باب طَلَاقِ الَّتِي لَمْ تَبْلُغْ وَ الَّتِي قَدْ يَئِسَتْ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 24 – Divorce of the woman who has yet to mature, and one who has despaired from the menstruation (menopause)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/145)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/145)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ فِي الْمَرْأَةِ الَّتِي قَدْ يَئِسَتْ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ قَالَ بَانَتْ مِنْهُ وَ لَا عِدَّةَ عَلَيْهَا .
وَ قَدْ رُوِيَ أَيْضاً أَنَّ عَلَيْهِنَّ الْعِدَّةَ إِذَا دُخِلَ بِهِنَّ .

en.hubeali: One of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Safwan, from Muhammad Bin Hakeem, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,
‘I heard Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> saying regarding the woman who has despaired from the menstruation: ‘She is irrevocably divorced from him, and there is no waiting period for her’.
And it has been reported as well that: ‘Upon her is the waiting period when she has been slept with’.
en.sarwar: 5. Certain persons of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from Safwan from Muhammad ibn Hakim from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I once heard abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘A woman after menopause if divorced becomes stranger to him and she does not need to count a waiting period.’” It is also narrated that if sexual intercourse has taken place with them there is a waiting period for them.
